title: Abqaiq/Khurais Attack → Oil Spike
summary: Drone and missile attacks on Saudi Arabia's Abqaiq processing facility and Khurais oil field temporarily removed approximately 5% of global oil supply, causing the largest single-day oil price spike in decades.
mechanism: A major attack on Saudi processing and output infrastructure temporarily removed a large share of global oil supply. The market priced both the immediate supply loss and elevated geopolitical risk premium.
causal_chain: attacks disable ~5.7 mb/d of Saudi processing capacity
market prices near-term supply loss and geopolitical escalation
crude risk premium rises sharply on Sunday open
oil benchmarks gap higher on Monday open

forecast.note: Bullish oil immediately; duration of impact and restoration timeline determines persistence

outcome.realized_note: Brent spiked as much as 19.5% intraday to $71.95. CNBC reported the largest single-day percentage gain in history. MacroTrends Brent chart shows 2019 high at $74.94.
outcome.chart_note: MacroTrends Brent chart confirms spike; CNBC reported 19.5% intraday move
outcome.reversal_note: Prices retraced significantly within days as Saudi Arabia signaled faster-than-expected restoration

sources.title: Oil price risk premium is back after attacks wipe out 5% of global supply
Oil drops 5% one day after historic surge
Brent Crude Oil Prices 10 Year Daily Chart

title: Freeport LNG Outage → Henry Hub Selloff
summary: An explosion at the Freeport LNG export terminal reduced U.S. LNG export capacity by ~2 Bcf/d, leaving more gas in the domestic market and sending Henry Hub sharply lower.
mechanism: The Freeport outage reduced U.S. LNG export demand, leaving more gas in the domestic balance and pushing Henry Hub lower. The mirror effect was supportive for European gas, which lost an import source.
causal_chain: Freeport LNG terminal shuts down after explosion
U.S. gas export capacity falls by ~2 Bcf/d
more gas remains in domestic U.S. balance
Henry Hub reprices lower
European TTF stays elevated as one LNG source is removed

forecast.note: Bearish Henry Hub while outage persists; bullish TTF/European gas as substitute

outcome.realized_note: EIA reported Henry Hub fell $1.27/MMBtu to $8.16 on June 9, and ended June at $6.54.
outcome.chart_note: MacroTrends natural gas chart shows 2022 volatile profile with summer decline
outcome.reversal_note: Henry Hub recovered partially when Freeport resumed partial operations in late 2022

sources.title: Freeport LNG outage shifts US supply and demand balance

title: Colonial Pipeline Ransomware Attack → U.S. Gasoline Shortage
summary: A ransomware attack forced Colonial Pipeline to shut down its main pipeline, causing fuel shortages across the U.S. East Coast and a spike in gasoline and diesel prices.
mechanism: Colonial Pipeline carries ~45% of fuel consumed on the U.S. East Coast. A shutdown of even a few days created localized supply stress and panic buying, driving up prices at the rack.
causal_chain: ransomware attack forces Colonial Pipeline to halt operations
~2.5 million b/d of refined product flow disrupted
U.S. East Coast fuel inventories begin drawing
panic buying at retail level amplifies supply stress
gasoline and diesel futures spike

forecast.note: Bullish East Coast gasoline and diesel; effect concentrated regionally

outcome.realized_note: RBOB gasoline futures spiked ~4-5%. Some gas stations ran out of fuel. Pipeline resumed within ~6 days.
outcome.chart_note: Provisional — gasoline futures chart confirms May 2021 spike
outcome.reversal_note: Prices normalized quickly as pipeline restarted within days

title: Nord Stream Pipeline Sabotage → European Gas Rally
summary: Sabotage explosions damaged three of the four strings of the Nord Stream 1 and 2 pipelines under the Baltic Sea, permanently removing Russian pipeline gas transit capacity to Europe.
mechanism: Although both pipelines were already not flowing commercially by September 2022 (Nord Stream 1 throttled, Nord Stream 2 never certified), the sabotage permanently removed the option of resumed Russian gas flows through these routes, reducing Europe's medium-term supply flexibility.
causal_chain: explosions damage Nord Stream pipelines at depth
three of four pipeline strings take on water and are rendered inoperable
market prices removal of any restart optionality
European gas prices spike on the news
EU energy security concerns deepen

forecast.note: Bullish European gas; removes optionality of Russian supply restoration through this route

outcome.realized_note: TTF spiked on the news but remained volatile. The fundamental impact was confirmatory rather than new information since flows were already minimal.
outcome.chart_note: Provisional — TTF chart confirms September 2022 spike on sabotage news
outcome.priced_in_note: Russian gas flows had already been near zero; much of the supply disruption was already priced
outcome.reversal_note: European gas prices declined sharply in late 2022 and 2023 as LNG imports rose and demand fell

title: Texas Winter Storm Uri → Natural Gas Spot Price Spike
summary: An unprecedented winter storm caused widespread power grid failures in Texas, freezing natural gas production and causing the Henry Hub spot price to spike to extreme levels.
mechanism: The simultaneous collapse of gas supply (frozen wellheads) and surge in heating demand during an extreme cold snap caused a localized physical shortage. Spot prices spiked to extraordinary levels before production recovered.
causal_chain: Winter Storm Uri brings record cold to Texas
gas wellheads, pipes and processing plants freeze
Texas gas supply falls by ~10-15 Bcf/d
heating demand surges simultaneously
Henry Hub spot prices spike to $100+/MMBtu in some regional hubs

forecast.note: Extremely bullish natural gas spot while freeze persists; effect concentrated in affected region

outcome.realized_note: Texas spot gas prices spiked to extreme levels. Waha (Texas hub) briefly reached $400+/MMBtu. Henry Hub prompt month spiked sharply. Widespread power outages for days.
outcome.chart_note: Provisional — Henry Hub chart confirms February 2021 spike
outcome.reversal_note: Prices normalized within days as temperatures recovered and production resumed

sources.title: Texas winter storm Uri natural gas pricing analysis

title: Libyan Civil War → Brent Oil Spike
summary: The outbreak of civil war in Libya in early 2011 removed nearly 1.5 million barrels per day of light sweet crude from the market, driving a sustained Brent rally and widening the WTI-Brent spread.
mechanism: Libya was a top-10 OPEC producer of light sweet crude especially valued by European refiners. Civil war production shutdown tightened the specific grade that European refineries were configured for.
causal_chain: civil unrest escalates to civil war in February 2011
oil production facilities evacuated and shut
Libyan output falls from ~1.5 mb/d to near zero
European refiners scramble for light sweet crude alternatives
Brent spikes toward $120; WTI-Brent spread widens

forecast.note: Bullish Brent specifically; light sweet crude grade tightness adds premium beyond headline Brent move

outcome.realized_note: Brent rallied from ~$100 to ~$120+ by April 2011. IEA released emergency stocks to help offset the supply loss.
outcome.chart_note: Provisional — Brent chart confirms H1 2011 elevated pricing above $110
outcome.reversal_note: Production eventually resumed partially in H2 2011 as rebels gained control

title: Ever Given Suez Canal Blockage → Oil and Freight Spike
summary: The Ever Given container ship ran aground in the Suez Canal in March 2021, blocking transit for six days and affecting ~12% of global trade flows.
mechanism: The Suez Canal handles ~12% of global trade and significant crude/LNG volumes. A blockage forces ships around the Cape of Good Hope, adding ~10 days and fuel costs. Short-term oil rally as supply routes lengthen and insurance/logistics costs rise.
causal_chain: Ever Given runs aground blocking Suez Canal
~400 ships queue on both ends of canal
crude tankers rerouted via Cape of Good Hope
additional fuel and time costs raise delivered prices
oil and freight briefly spike on supply logistics concern

forecast.note: Mildly bullish crude and freight; impact limited by expectation of quick resolution

outcome.realized_note: Brent spiked ~4-5% on the event but retraced quickly once the channel was reopened after 6 days. Freight rates saw modest upward pressure.
outcome.chart_note: Provisional — Brent chart shows brief March 2021 bump
outcome.reversal_note: Canal unblocked March 29; prices fully normalized within days. Insurance premiums lingered.

sources.title: Suez Canal Ever Given grounding coverage

title: Niger Delta Militant Attacks → Nigerian Output Fall and Brent Support
summary: Renewed militant attacks on oil infrastructure in Nigeria's Niger Delta in early 2016 cut Nigerian crude output by ~400,000-700,000 b/d, contributing to Brent's recovery from multi-year lows.
mechanism: Nigeria is a significant light sweet crude producer and OPEC member. Militant attacks on pipelines and loading facilities repeatedly disrupted output below the country's OPEC quota, tightening the West African light sweet market.
causal_chain: Niger Delta Avengers begin pipeline and facility attacks
Nigerian crude production falls by ~400-700 kb/d
force majeure declared on several cargoes
West African light sweet crude tightens
Brent supported above OPEC-era lows

forecast.note: Supportive for Brent; magnitude depends on how long militant activity persists and whether alternative light sweet origins can fill gap

outcome.realized_note: Nigerian production fell to ~30-year lows in 2016. Brent recovered from ~$27 lows in January 2016 to ~$55 by year-end, assisted by Nigeria shortfall plus OPEC discussions.
outcome.chart_note: Provisional — Brent chart shows 2016 recovery from January lows
outcome.priced_in_note: OPEC Vienna agreement in November 2016 was the dominant driver of the full-year Brent recovery
